objective: Fix prompt trust boundaries, remove hard assistant-turn truncation, fold repair instructions into the canonical prompt contract, and widen prompt fingerprint coverage.
scope_summary: Hardened the universal prompt contract and LangGraph assistant-turn loop so trusted instructions stay in the system role, untrusted transcript/tool context stays in the lower-priority context envelope, protocol repair guidance is contract-owned, and prompt observability covers the reusable full prompt surface.

changes.summary: Moved untrusted reply-target, transcript, and tool-observation data out of the system message and into the tagged lower-priority context envelope, and widened prompt fingerprinting to cover the reusable system-plus-envelope template.
Removed the fixed last-eight-message assistant-turn cap and routed protocol-repair guidance through canonical graph state plus trusted runtime state in the prompt contract.
Updated transcript and prompt-contract coverage so the tests assert the new system-vs-user trust boundary and the absence of hard replay clipping.
Updated architecture and changelog docs to describe the new trust boundary, fingerprint scope, and transcript continuity behavior.

validations.notes: Artifact JSON validated after the completion plan/report were updated.
Workflow audit passed after the updated artifacts were written.
ESLint passed with zero warnings after the transcript test adjustments.
Markdown lint and docs link checks passed with the updated architecture wording.

evidence: Full repo test suite passed: 120 files, 700 tests.
TypeScript build passed after the prompt-contract and graph-state changes.
Docs lint and link checks passed with the updated architecture wording.
decisions: Kept protocol-repair guidance as trusted runtime state so repair instructions stay within the canonical prompt contract without promoting untrusted data back into the system role.
Fingerprinting now models the reusable prompt template instead of hashing per-turn runtime content, while still varying with prompt mode and active tool surface.
risks: This pass did not add a model-in-the-loop adversarial eval harness; prompt-injection and tool-choice behavior are still covered by unit and transcript tests rather than live eval scoring.
